blocks:
سُورة التكوير

مكّيّة وهي 29 آية

سورة كوّرت

مكيّة وهي تسع وعشرون آية بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) إِذَا الشَّمْسُ كُوِّرَتْ ** (1) When the sun is wrapped up, (Its light is) wrapped up, so its spreading across the horizons departs. Al-Qummi said: It will become black and dark. (٢) وَإِذَا النُّجُومُ انْكَدَرَتْ ** (2) and when the stars fall, He said: Their light will depart. (٣) وَإِذَا الْجِبالُ سُيِّرَتْ ** (3) and when the mountains are set in motion, He said: They will be set in motion, just as He said: تَحْسَبُها جامِدَةً وَهِيَ تَمُرُّ مَرَّ السَّحابِ. (You will think them to be stationary, yet they will move like the passing of the clouds.) (٤) وَإِذَا الْعِشارُ ** (4) and when the pregnant camels are abandoned, The she-camels that have completed ten months of their pregnancy (plural of عشراء) are abandoned. Al-Qummi said: The camels will be abandoned when creation dies, and there will be no one left to milk them. (٥) وَإِذَا الْوُحُوشُ حُشِرَتْ ** (5) and when the wild beasts are mustered, They are gathered from every side, or resurrected/raised. (٦) وَإِذَا الْبِحارُ سُجِّرَتْ ** (6) and when the seas are set ablaze, He said: The seas that surround the entire world will all turn into fires. And it was recited سجرت (with the lightened form). (٧) وَإِذَا النُّفُوسُ زُوِّجَتْ ** (7) and when the souls are paired [with their bodies], He said: With the wide-eyed حور (maidens of Paradise). And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): As for the people of Paradise, they will be paired with the good and beautiful ones (الخيرات الحسان). And as for the people of the Fire, with every person among them is a devil. Meaning: The souls of the disbelievers and the hypocrites will be paired with the devils, so they are their companions (قرناؤهم).

(٨) وَإِذَا الْمَوْؤُدَةُ سُئِلَتْ. (8) And when the girl buried alive is questioned, (٩) بِأَيِّ ذَنْبٍ قُتِلَتْ ** (9) for what sin she was killed. meaning that the girl buried alive is questioned about the reason for her killing, as a rebuke (تبكيتاً) to the one who buried her (لوائدها). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: The Arabs used to kill daughters out of jealousy (للغيرة). When the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) comes, the girl buried alive (الموؤدة) will be questioned: بِأَيِّ ذَنْبٍ قُتِلَتْ.** (for what sin she was killed.)

And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): (It is read) with the fathah on the mīm and the wāw. And he said: What is meant by that is the womb (الرّحم) and kinship (القرابة), and that the one who severs it (قاطعها) will be questioned about the reason for severing it.

And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means the kinship (قرابة) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and those killed in Jihad (جهاد). And in another narration, he said: It refers to those killed due to our love (مودّتنا) and our guardianship (ولايتنا).

And Al-Qummi (القمّيّ), narrated from him (peace be upon him), said: Those killed due to our love (مودّتنا).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: He (Allah) says: I question you concerning the affection (المودّة) whose virtue was revealed to you—the affection of the near kin (مودّة ذي القربى)—**بِأَيِّ ذَنْبٍ ** (for what sin) did you kill them?

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The like of it.

(١٠) وَإِذَا الصُّحُفُ نُشِرَتْ ** (10) when the scrolls are unrolled, Al-Qummi said: [These are] the scrolls of deeds. And it was recited with al-tashdid (doubling the letter). (١١) وَإِذَا السَّماءُ كُشِطَتْ ** (11) when the heaven is stripped away, It is pulled off and removed. Al-Qummi said: It is nullified (أُبْطِلَتْ). (١٢) وَإِذَا الْجَحِيمُ سُعِّرَتْ ** (12) when hell is set ablaze, It is kindled with an intense kindling. (١٣) وَإِذَا الْجَنَّةُ أُزْلِفَتْ ** (13) and when paradise is brought near, It is brought near to the believers. (١٤) عَلِمَتْ نَفْسٌ ما أَحْضَرَتْ ** (14) every soul will know what it has prepared. [This is] the answer (جَوَاب) to [the preceding] ** وَإِذَا . (١٥) فَلا أُقْسِمُ بِالْخُنَّسِ ** (15) I swear by the receding stars, Al-Qummi said: That is, أُقْسِمُ بِالْخُنَّسِ ** (I swear by the receding stars), and they are the name for the stars (النُّجُوم). And in al-Majma': They are the stars (النُّجُوم) that recede (تَخْنِسُ) during the day and appear (تَبْدُو) at night. And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): They are five stars (أَنْجُم): Saturn (زُحَل), Jupiter (الْمُشْتَرِي), Mars (الْمِرِّيخ), Venus (الزُّهْرَة), and Mercury (الْعُطَارِد). I say: And for this reason, they were described by the term al-jawāz (passing/movement), for these five are the wandering, receding planets (السَّيَّارَات الرَّوَاجِع). This supports what was said: that بِالْخُنَّسِ ** is in the meaning of al-rawāji' (the receding/returning ones), derived from khanasa (خَنَسَ) when it means to lag behind (تَأَخَّرَ).

(١٦) الْجَوارِ ** (The running ones) The moving ones (السيّارات), running in their orbits.

الْكُنَّسِ ** (The sweeping ones) It was said: those that are concealed (المتواريات) beneath the light of the sun. And Al-Qummi said: The stars hide (تكنس) during the day, so they are not visible.

And in Al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him), that He was asked about them, and He said: "An Imam who hides (يخنس) in the year two hundred and sixty, then He appears like a meteor (الشهاب) blazing in the dark night, and if you reach His time, your eye will be cooled."

And in Al-Ikmal, there is what is close to it.

وَاللَّيْلِ إِذا عَسْعَسَ (١٧) (and by the night when it draws to a close,) Its darkness approached or departed, and it is among the (الأضداد) (words having opposite meanings). In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "When it departs with its darkness." And al-Qummi said: "When it darkens."

وَالصُّبْحِ إِذا تَنَفَّسَ (١٨) (and by the dawn when it brightens:) He said: "When it rises." It was said: He expressed the rising (بالتنفّس) (by breathing) to mean the approach of spirit (روح) and breeze (نسيم).

إِنَّهُ لَقَوْلُ رَسُولٍ كَرِيمٍ (١٩) (it is indeed the speech of a noble apostle,) Meaning, the Quran is the speech of a noble apostle, meaning Gabriel (جبرئيل), for he spoke on behalf of Allah.

ذِي قُوَّةٍ عِنْدَ ذِي الْعَرْشِ مَكِينٍ (٢٠) (possessing power, established with the Lord of the Throne,) With Allah, possessing high standing (ذي مكانة).

مُطاعٍ ثَمَّ أَمِينٍ (٢١) (obeyed there, and trustworthy.) Obeyed among His angels, there, trustworthy concerning the Revelation (الوحي). And (ثَمَ) (there) specifies its connection to what precedes it and what follows it. In al-Majma', in the narration: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to Gabriel (جبرئيل): "How excellent is the praise your Lord bestowed upon you: possessing power, established with the Lord of the Throne, obeyed there, and trustworthy. What was your power, and what was your trustworthiness?" He replied: "As for my power, I was sent to the cities of Lot (لوط), and they were four cities, in each city four hundred thousand fighters, excluding the children. I lifted them from the lowest earth until the inhabitants of the heavens heard the sounds of the chickens and the howling of the dogs. Then I plunged them down and overturned them. As for my trustworthiness, I was never commanded to do something and then transgressed it to something else." And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He said to Gabriel (جبرئيل) when وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِلْعَالَمِينَ (We have not sent you except as a mercy to all the nations) was revealed: "Did any part of this mercy reach you?" He replied: "Yes. I used to fear the outcome of the matter, so I attained security through you when Allah praised me with His saying: possessing power, established with the Lord of the Throne."

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: possessing power, established with the Lord of the Throne. He said: "He means Gabriel (جبرئيل)." It was asked: "What about His saying: obeyed there, and trustworthy?" He replied: "He means the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. He is the one obeyed by his Lord, the trustworthy one on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة)."

(٢٢) وَما صاحِبُكُمْ بِمَجْنُونٍ ** (22) (Your companion is not mad.) He said: meaning the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله regarding his establishment of the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام as a standard (علماً) for the people. I say: It is a refutation of what the hypocrites (المنافقون) falsely accused him of.

(٢٣) ** وَلَقَدْ رَآهُ ** (23) (He certainly saw him) It was said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله certainly saw Gabriel (جبرئيل) **بِالْأُفُقِ الْمُبِينِ ** (on the manifest horizon), at the highest rising place of the sun.

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked about بِالْأُفُقِ الْمُبِينِ ** (the manifest horizon). He said: It is a plain (قاع) before the Throne (العرش) in which there are rivers flowing, and in it are cups (القدحان) equal to the number of the stars.

(٢٤)** وَما هُوَ عَلَى الْغَيْبِ بِظَنِينٍ ** (24) And he is not stingy about the Unseen.

It was said: And Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله عَلَى الْغَيْبِ ** regarding what He informs of the revelation and other things, is not بِظنِينٍ (accused (بمتّهم)) from aẓ-ẓinnah (الظنّة), which is the accusation (التهمة).

And it was read with the letter ḍād (الضّاد) from aḍ-ḍann (الضّنّ), which is stinginess (البخل), meaning: He does not withhold (لا يبخل) the conveyance (التبليغ) and the teaching (التعليم).

And Al-Qummi narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: وَما هُوَ ** (And He), may He be blessed and exalted, is not بِضَنِينٍ ** with His Unseen (بغيبه) towards His Prophet (عليه).

(٢٥) ** وَما هُوَ بِقَوْلِ شَيْطانٍ رَجِيمٍ ** (25) nor is it the saying of a rejected devil. He said: meaning the soothsayers (الكهنة) who were in Quraysh. So He attributed their speech to the speech of the devils (الشياطين) who were with them, speaking upon their tongues. So He said: ** وَما هُوَ بِقَوْلِ شَيْطانٍ رَجِيمٍ ** (nor is it the saying of a rejected devil) like those (soothsayers).

(٢٦) فَأَيْنَ تَذْهَبُونَ ** (26) So where are you going? He said: "Where are you going concerning Ali (peace be upon him)?" meaning: "Where are you fleeing from his guardianship (ولايته)?" (٢٧) إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِلْعالَمِينَ ** (27) It is nothing but a reminder for all the nations. He said: "For those from whom Allah took the covenant (ميثاقه) regarding his guardianship (ولايته)." (٢٨) لِمَنْ شاءَ مِنْكُمْ أَنْ يَسْتَقِيمَ ** (28) for those of you who desire to be upright. He said: "Regarding the obedience (طاعة) to Ali (peace be upon him) and the Imams (peace be upon them) from after him." (٢٩) وَما تَشاؤُنَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ رَبُّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (29) But you will not desire unless Allah, the Lord of all the nations, desires. He said: "Because the Will (المشيئة) belongs to Him, may He be blessed and exalted, not to the people." And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah made the hearts of the Imams (peace be upon them) the locus (مورداً) of His Will (إرادته). So, if Allah wills something, they will it. And this is His saying: وَما تَشاؤُنَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ رَبُّ الْعالَمِينَ (But you will not desire unless Allah, the Lord of all the nations, desires)." And the reward (ثواب) for reciting the Surah has already preceded in Surah 'Abasa wa Tawalla.
